title: Why Planet Labs (PL) Stock Is Trading Up Today
text:
Planet Labs stock continues to trade higher, driven by multiple positive catalysts including recent contract announcements and strong financial performance. The latest surge reflects a seven-figure satellite imagery contract with the Greek government, supported by the European Space Agency. This deal adds to the company's impressive pipeline of government contracts across Europe and North America.

The company's fiscal 2026 results provided concrete evidence of successful execution, with Q4 revenue of $86.8 million exceeding expectations by 11.55%. Full-year revenue of $307.7 million represented 26% growth with the first full year of non-GAAP profitability. The company's transition to positive free cash flow ($52.9 million) and strong adjusted EBITDA ($15.5 million) demonstrates sustainable business fundamentals.

Recent announcements include the successful launch and operation of nine Pelican satellites with first-light imagery releases demonstrating the fleet's capabilities. The company's 32-satellite constellation vision aims to image any Earth location up to 30 times daily at 30-centimeter resolution. Each satellite carries NVIDIA's Jetson AI platform, enabling real-time processing and intelligence generation at the edge.

The market appears to be recognizing the company's transformation from a pre-profitability venture to a profitable growth company with substantial backlog and strong international demand. Contract wins with Sweden, Germany, Greece, and other nations validate the global appeal of Planet Labs' services. Analyst upgrades and price target increases reflect confidence in the company's ability to sustain growth while maintaining profitability.
